column2: MIS for reporting on Cadebill Billing for CX Success

Today, Services teams have functionality that accrues and decrements PS Project and PSoD hours to their account based off their purchased success package (although it is not functioning; SF team working on a fix to the VisualForce page now).  This functionality/automation exists within the PSOD/Project Hours Allocation button located on the account page of a given customer.  The automation only tracks allocated and used time, but does feed to Cadebill when hours are used (this is done manually for PSoD hours and not tracked for PS Project Hours).  For this enhancement, we want to automate the billing aspect when hours are used from this allocation.  KB Process articles below (for today’s handling)
PS OnDemand Billing & Time Logging Procedures: https://niceincontact.my.salesforce.com/kA139000000H6Jz?srPos=1&srKp=ka1&lang=en_US

PS Project Hours – no documented process available; the teams track these hours in an excel doc to manage their accrual and usage month over month (the expectation is that this enhancement will allow the teams to eliminate the manual tracking aspect and rely on the PSOD/Project Hours Allocation button to track available hours.
Invoice display requirements
Invoice should display PSoD charge with SOW reference on invoice when utilized
If PSoD hours are available for use on the customer’s account, these should be decremented (from the PSOD/Project Hours Allocation button) and displayed as $0 charge on the invoice
Invoice should display PS Project hours charge with SOW reference on invoice when utilized
If PS Project hours are available for use on the customer’s account, these should be decremented (from the PSOD/Project Hours Allocation button) and displayed as $0 charge on the invoice
Start to End workflow expectations
Customer Accrues PSoD/PS Project Hours based off their Success Package
Customer Uses hours for project
Hours logged to PSOD/Project Hours Allocation button by Professional Services or Technical Success
If hours are available, they are decremented from the allocation
If hours are not available, they will be billed to the customer at the correct rate (based off qty of hours)

Need to verify that Cadebill adjusts the billing rate by customer (we have some customers with grandfathered rates; Cadebill does currently account for this when calculating the billing total based off hours entered)
Customer receives invoice
Invoice displays the hours billed with reference to the Project ID/SOW (see attached example, this is done for PSoD hours today, but not PS Project Hours)
If hours were used from their available allocation, a credit would display to offset the charges on the invoice
End

433678 - Committed Quantity Automation Business Rule,CXMIS-19121,844502,Epic,In Development,CXMIS,CXMIS,software,Chanchal Katiyar,6405e9049ce2cd2c240f713a,,,P3,,,,srv Jira,712020:fbe734e5-99e6-46ff-b65c-bcc60d1677cc,srv Jira,712020:fbe734e5-99e6-46ff-b65c-bcc60d1677cc,17/Feb/26 1:25 PM,05/May/26 2:53 PM,17/Jun/26 1:29 PM,,,,,,23/Apr/26 12:00 AM,0,,,,"When we launched the automation we faced a challenge. The underlying rule was that SOFTW and SOFTU (Software MRC & Usage) will be transferred with a quantity of 1. But there were several exceptions – for specific SKUs and specific SKUs per specific partner (RingCentral & Verizon).
There was no SF identifier for the relevant SKUs, so we relied on SKU External Description and asked MCL to create a business rule for all the SKUs which should be entered with quoted quantity.
Maintaining this list would be a nightmare and prone to errors.
Since then, we moved RingCentral automation to rely on Unit of Measure Business Rule.
For each SKU, Workato will “look” at the SKU page and for identified Unit of Measure and Partner RingCentral, will transfer the quoted quantity
Now, SF has created a field on the SKU level, like Unit of Measure, defining whether the SKU should be billed by contracted quantity or higher or normally according to usage.

SKU API : Contracted_QTY__c",,srv Jira,,,,,712020:fbe734e5-99e6-46ff-b65c-bcc60d1677cc,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Create a new generic Business Rule: For Product Family “Software MRC” or “Software Usage” Workato will look at the SKU “Contracted_QTY__c” and if True will transfer to Cadebill the quoted “Quantity”In Parallel, create “Verizon Contracted Quantity” BR.If opp Partner Account INCLUDES Verizon ANDAccount “Government Account” is FALSEThen for selected SKUs (existing BR SKUs under Verizon) => enter “Quantity” to CadebillOnce this is in place, the existing Contracted Quantity BR can be retired  (you can build #2 based on it as well, whatever works)  After that, whenever CPQ defines a new SKU, it will determine whether it is committed quantity True.https://nice-it.atlassian.net/browse/IT-29665",,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,Jonathan Jensen,712020:4c7bb93a-69d9-47b6-bc60-f97d9b955661,,,,,,,,Finance,robert.gordon,712020:df79a8f6-847c-4b10-a993-274b231e4ebc,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,{},,CX,,,,,,,,,ghx-label-3,,,To Do,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

Minimum‑Related Case Intake and Routing,CXSCOE-10332,858637,Epic,Design,CXSCOE,CXSCOE,software,Devin Jefferies,611cf68ca3e00f00687a04e0,,,P3,,,,srv Jira,712020:fbe734e5-99e6-46ff-b65c-bcc60d1677cc,srv Jira,712020:fbe734e5-99e6-46ff-b65c-bcc60d1677cc,08/May/26 4:54 PM,08/Jun/26 4:11 PM,09/Jun/26 4:53 PM,,,,,,,0,,,,"Sales representatives currently submit minimum‑related inquiries through existing Sales Operations case channels, which results in mixed routing, delayed response times, and lack of ownership clarity. Finance leadership has requested a more structured approach to ensure that minimum‑specific inquiries are handled separately and efficiently.
A separate case submission path for minimum‑related inquiries, or
Enhanced routing logic within existing Sales Ops cases to automatically identify and route minimum‑related cases.

Additionally, Orr has requested the creation of a dedicated case queue for minimum inquiries, which sales representatives can use when opening cases. These cases will route directly to James Boase for handling.

Due to the need for Salesforce configuration and/or development, this work must be initiated as a formal MIS project and aligned with Finance priorities, as confirmed by Robert.",,srv Jira,,,,,712020:fbe734e5-99e6-46ff-b65c-bcc60d1677cc,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Business GoalProvide a clear, efficient, and Finance-aligned way for Sales Representatives to submit minimum-related inquiries in Salesforce and ensure those cases are routed correctly without manual intervention.Customer Need:A clear submission path for minimum-related casesCorrect and consistent routing aligned to Finance ownershipReduced delays and rework caused by manual reassignmentVisibility and reporting on minimum-related inquiriesElimination of manual triage burden on Sales Operations and TAM teamsThis request was raised by Orr and is confirmed as part of Finance priorities by Robert.Solution DetailsThe proposed solution is to enhance the existing Salesforce case intake flow to support minimum-specific case handling. Based on the May 28 discovery discussion, the preferred approach is to piggyback on the existing Sales Operations case process rather than create a new case type or separate submission path. This avoids architectural complexity while achieving the desired routing outcome.At a high level, the solution will:Provide Sales Representatives with a clear method to submit cases related to minimums within the existing Sales Ops case submission flowIntroduce a new initial categorization option (e.g., “Contracted Minimum Inquiry”) within the existing case submission flowAutomatically route minimum-related cases to a dedicated Minimum Inquiries queue, owned by James BoaseKeep the case record type as Sales Ops (consistent with the existing model, similar to CPQ Admin queue escalations)Implementation ApproachThe existing Sales Ops case submission experience is a guided flow (not direct Case object creation). Sales reps access it via a button on the relevant Salesforce record (Opportunity, Account, or Quote). The flow presents context-specific initial categorization options based on the record type selected.Technical DetailsHigh-level technical considerations include:Salesforce Case ConfigurationCase Record Type:No new case record type required. Cases will remain Sales Ops record type, consistent with the existing model.New initial categorization option added to the Sales Ops case submission flow under the 'Account' optionOption label to be confirmed “Contracted Minimum Inquiry” under the 'Initial Categorization' drop-down﻿Routing & AssignmentCreate a new dedicated Minimum Inquiries queue in SalesforceQueue ownership assigned to James BoaseAutomated assignment rule or flow logic to route cases with the new categorization to this queueManual fallback: if a case is misdirected, Sales Ops teams (Angie’s and Hugo’s) and James Boase can manually reassign, consistent with current practiceAutomationDeclarative automation preferred (e.g., Record-Triggered Flow or Assignment Rule)Logic to detect the “Contracted Minimum Inquiry” initial categorization and route to the Minimum Inquiries queueNo manual queue management required by Sales Ops teams for correctly submitted casesReporting & VisibilityCases in the Minimum Inquiries queue will be filterable and reportable independently from standard Sales Ops casesThe Minimum Inquiries queue is expected to be excluded from Sales Ops SLA reporting in the same manner as CPQ Admin queue escalations are currently excludedFinance oversight and prioritization of minimum inquiries supported through queue-based reportingGovernanceConfiguration-first approach; custom development only if required following architectural reviewMust adhere to MIS development and deployment standardsAngie Draper and Hugo Pineda (Sales Ops team leads) to review and sign off on the design before implementation proceedsStakeholdersRequestor: OrrFinance Priority Owner: Jason W.Queue Owner: James BoaseImpacted Teams:FinanceSales OperationsMIS / Salesforce Administration",,,,,,,,,,,,,,jonathan.jensen,712020:0ccf8b0f-2002-4a3d-bc14-ae34e9bc6d3c,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Finance,srv Jira,712020:fbe734e5-99e6-46ff-b65c-bcc60d1677cc,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,{},47.0,CX,,,,,,,,,ghx-label-7,,,To Do,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

Minimum Usage Report Enhancements - Future Minimum and Ranking Logic,IT-34067,863767,Change Request,Open,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P1,,Yana Gurevich,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,Idan Katz,712020:8911770d-23b1-48f3-bf4c-ecfe5f31db3c,Idan Katz,712020:8911770d-23b1-48f3-bf4c-ecfe5f31db3c,01/Jun/26 6:33 PM,03/Jun/26 8:01 PM,,,,,,GRO Requirements 2026,,0,,,,"h3. Background / Business Need
Currently, the billing system does not correctly handle two edge cases in minimum usage fee allocation:
# Customers without a current contractual minimum may have a *future minimum commitment* that should be reflected in billing
# The *parent-level minimum* allocation logic lacks a defined ranking method for distributing charges across Business Units (BUs)
This CR addresses both gaps to ensure accurate billing categorization and reporting.
h4. 1. Future Minimum — Customers Without Current Contractual Minimum
* When a customer has *no active contractual minimum*, the system must check whether a *future minimum commitment* exists
* If a future minimum is found → present the billing amount under *""Amount over the Minimum""* (instead of the current behavior: *""No Minimum""*)
* If no future minimum exists → Need to check number 2.
h4. 2. Parent Minimum — BU Ranking & Charge Split Logic
* Implement a *ranking method* to determine the order in which BU charges are applied against the parent-level minimum
* The ranking is based on the *account creation date in Cadebill*.
** The account record type of the Parent account should be *Customers*

** When a *minimum is defined* on the *Parent Account* and *“Included Child Accounts” is marked*, only the *parent account* contract minimum should be considered. (Need to *ignore* the child account contractual minimum if exists)
* Charges must be correctly split between:
** *""Amount up to the Minimum""* — usage counted toward fulfilling the committed minimum
** *""Amount over the Minimum""* — usage exceeding the committed minimum
*The parent account is JOHNSON CONTROLS INC GLOBAL with Contractual Minimum of 150,000*
|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC BSNA|7|2026-Q3|         100,000|Amount over the Minimum|
|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC EMEA|6|2026-Q1|         100,000|Amount over the Minimum|
|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC UKI|5|2025|         100,000|Amount over the Minimum|
|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC NEW ZEALAND AUS|4|2024|         100,000|Amount over the Minimum|
|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC GLOBAL|3|2023|         100,000|Amount over the Minimum|
|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC|JOHNSON CONTROLS PREVIEW INSTANCE|2|2022|         100,000|Amount up to the Minimum|
|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC|JOHNSON CONTROLS INC GLOBAL|1|2021|           50,000|Amount up to the Minimum|
If items 1 and 2 do not apply, the billing amount should be presented under *""No Minimum”*
* Incorrect ranking logic could misallocate charges between ""Amount up to Minimum"" and ""Amount over Minimum,"" impacting dashboard accuracy
* Define and confirm BU ranking criteria with business stakeholders
* Implement future minimum lookup in billing engine
* Update allocation category logic per the decision tree above
* Validate against JOHNSON CONTROLS INC example data
* Regression test existing minimum and no-minimum customer scenarios

* Update billing report output to reflect new categories",,Idan Katz,,,,,712020:8911770d-23b1-48f3-bf4c-ecfe5f31db3c,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Sales,Michael Lozover,611ddcd4c2f3a50069f7e1af,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,{},,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

need to make sure it does not impact the invoice ",,Meital Rashti,,,,,70121:33c9c08c-01ca-4a34-ac93-994a2946b894,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,07/May/26 7:52 AM;ug:09c91872-a878-4f07-97a1-3f1811d26aca;IT-32336_Track_User_Fields_v1.0.docx;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/184808,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Billing,Meital Rashti,70121:33c9c08c-01ca-4a34-ac93-994a2946b894,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,{},,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"*Workato Recipe Logic:*

# Workato Recipe 56162808 (SF2CB Amendments) — before each Track_User_Fields API call to Cadebill (table: customer_product_instance), a conditional step checks whether the product's start_date is greater than the requested end_date.
# If start_date > end_date (or start_date = end_date), the recipe overrides end_date = start_date + 1 day before sending the payload to Cadebill.
# If start_date <= end_date (product is active or end date is valid), no override is applied and the original end_date is used.

Option chosen for Dispatch method are *_E-mail_*, *_E-mail & Post_* and *_Customer Portal & Post_*.

Setting the Dispatch Method to ‘None’ for int’l accounts will solve the issue however ‘Dispatch method’ is set only to filter the records. Setting ‘_Dispatch method’_ *_None_*  doesn’t mean invoices can’t be send from Cadebill.
If option ‘_Do Not Send Invoice’_ is set as TRUE, it means invoices won’t be send from Cadebill.

However, there is a restriction in Cadebill that doesn’t allow stand-alone accounts to have this option checked, that’s why you see the error *‘The account xxxxxx does not have any sub accounts or is not a child of another account’* . It is a preventive measure taken to avoid the situation where it is accidentally checked by the agent and customer don’t receive the invoice.
If needed, we can try checking this option using scripts/job.

Fix instance status handling for overlapping Maintenance Windows ,IT-34275,864824,Change Request,Design,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,08/Jun/26 4:18 PM,17/Jun/26 12:18 PM,,,July 2026,,,,,0,,,,"h2. *Summary*
Enhance Maintenance Window logic to align case suppression and instance sub-status with Change Request lifecycle

The current Maintenance Window logic sets the instance sub-status (In maintenance) based on the start and end of the maintenance window itself. This approach creates inaccuracies when multiple maintenance windows overlap on the same instance or when multiple change requests occur at different times within a maintenance.
To address this, the logic should be updated so that instance sub-status and case suppression are driven by the lifecycle of associated Change Requests rather than the maintenance window duration.
During analysis, it was identified that:

* An instance can be part of multiple maintenance windows simultaneously. When one maintenance ends while another is still active, the system removes the sub-status incorrectly and resumes case creation.
* Alerts triggered during ongoing work can therefore generate unwanted cases, even though maintenance activity is still in progress.
* A single maintenance window may include multiple change requests with different execution times.
* Current behavior does not reflect the actual work timeline at the change level, leading to inaccurate monitoring suppression.
Update system behavior to align instance sub-status and case suppression with Change Request lifecycle:
#* When a Change Request moves to *“Implement”*, apply sub-status (Downtime CR) to relevant instances.
#* When the Change Request moves to *“Review”*, remove the sub-status.
#* Remove existing logic that applies/removes sub-status based on maintenance window timing.
#* When attempting to end a maintenance:
#** If any associated Change Request is still in “Implement”:
#*** Display a warning notification to the user “Please note, CR # (can be multiples) is still in Implement state”
#** Change Requests are not moved to “Implement” after maintenance start.
#** Maintenance is being closed while active changes still exist.
#* Validation applies *only to Change Requests linked to the maintenance window*, not unrelated changes.
IMPORTANT! Please note: when we built the CR substatus with Tzeela (CR 19130) we exclude CR related to MW, we just need to remove this exclusion and it will work.
h3. *AC1 – Sub-status driven by Change Request*
* GIVEN a Change Request linked to a maintenance
* THEN the instance sub-status is set to Downtime CR (like any other CR)
* THEN the instance sub-status is removed
* GIVEN a maintenance window starts or ends
* THEN no sub-status changes occur unless triggered by a Change Request
* GIVEN an instance has at least one Change in “Implement”
* THEN alerts do NOT generate cases
* GIVEN a user attempts to end a maintenance
* AND at least one Change is still in “Implement”
** Shows a warning requiring user acknowledgment
h3. *AC5 – No Impact from Overlapping Maintenance*
* GIVEN multiple maintenance windows exist on the same instance
* THEN sub-status behavior is controlled only by Change Requests
* GIVEN Change Requests not linked to the maintenance
* THEN they are NOT considered in validation or sub-status logic
* when a MW is move to Start and End there is a warning to say that Instances will be in sub-status, need to remove that, and add a sentence to MWM to ensure that users will change CR to implement.
* The exact sentence that need to be removed is: the creation of cases will be disabled from alert/alarm

* the exact sentence that need to be is: Please note on changing the status to start maintenance, the publication is going to be sent to the publication customers-related list, the publication will be displayed in the portal and under the instances, and a message will be sent to the Change request owner, please check the CR owner move the status to disable creation of cases
** Eliminates false case creation during ongoing maintenance work
** Improves monitoring accuracy and alert suppression
** Fully aligns with standard Change Management lifecycle (Implement → Review).
** Very large maintenance scopes (e.g., thousands of CIs) may impact performance
** Users must follow correct Change lifecycle updates to avoid unintended alerts

** Manage all related work within a single maintenance window to avoid duplicate behavior issues.",,Dany Ditchi,,,,,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,SNOW Dev,,,,,,,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,ALM - Cross Domains,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,{},,CX,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"*CR Sub-status Logic:*

# Remove the MW-exclusion condition from the existing Business Rule / Script Include (built in IT-19130) that prevents MW-linked CRs from triggering the 'Downtime CR' sub-status flow. After removal, when a CR linked to a Maintenance Window transitions to 'Implement', sub-status 'Downtime CR' is set on all related instances — identical to non-MW CRs.

# When a MW-linked CR transitions to 'Review', sub-status is removed from all related instances. If multiple CRs cover the same instance, sub-status is only cleared when no remaining linked CR is in 'Implement' state.

# Remove or disable sub-status logic from the Script Actions 'Set Instances In Maintenance' and 'Clear Instances In Maintenance' (NiCE Publication Page, identified in IT-23157). MW Start/End transitions must no longer apply or remove any instance sub-status.
# Review Script Include 'RetrieveMaintenance' for any sub-status-related logic that must be removed or updated.

# Add a UI Action / Client Script on cmn_schedule_maintenance: when the user attempts to transition to 'End Maintenance', query all linked CRs in 'Implement' status. If any exist, display a warning dialog: 'Please note, CR #[numbers] is/are still in Implement state'. User must acknowledge before proceeding. No warning is shown when no linked CRs are in Implement.

# Remove the sub-status notification/warning from the existing 'Start Maintenance' and 'End Maintenance' action messages. Add an informational guidance note on the MWM form: 'Once maintenance begins, move linked Change Requests to Implement to activate monitoring suppression.'",FALSE,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Wiser,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Wiser,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,New,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,1|i0bmgr:,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Dany Ditchi,,,,,,,Run the Business,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,IT S64 (1 Jun - 15 Jun 2026),IT S65 (15 Jun - 29 Jun 2026),,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,3.0,,,,,,,,,,,,,Customer Support,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,To Do,08/Jun/26 4:18 PM

Automatic User Creation in Wiser,IT-33005,854476,Change Request,Design,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,09/Apr/26 12:48 PM,15/Jun/26 3:37 PM,,,,,,,,0,On_Hold,,,"+New Flow:+

# Once the Request ticket is opened in the NSC (“Request for Wiser permission”), an existing automated approval process is triggered that sends the request for approval per Division (the Division information is available under the user profile in ServiceNow).
NSC KW (#KB0011070) containing all approval users per Division. This step already exist and no need to develop it.
Send API from SNOW to IDM to create the user automatically in Wiser (send: Employee number and User type)
* IDM validates if the user already exists.
* If not exist, IDM creates the user.
* If exist, IDM update permissions (example: from fulfiller to Manager).
** If Failure, the task is routed to GSD to handle manually.
*** User will be created automatically in Wiser with SSO login
*** NSC request will be updated accordingly.
Once the existing number of users is above the baseline limit (limit will be kept in new parameters), users will not be able to be created automatically or maybe the Approver might be notified (?):
# Existing 'Request for Wiser permission' NSC catalog item and division-based automated approval (KB0011070) used unchanged — no development required for this step.

# On ticket approval, NSC fires a webhook/outbound REST message to Workato recipe 'Wiser User Provisioning'. Payload: employee number, user type, requester division (from NSC user profile), NSC ticket sys_id.

# Workato reads the division baseline system parameter (u_wiser_actimize_baseline / u_wiser_ps_baseline / u_wiser_cx_baseline) and compares against active Wiser user count for that division. If count >= baseline: provisioning blocked, NSC ticket updated (exact behaviour per OI-01 resolution). If within limit: proceeds to IDM call.
# Workato calls IDM/NetIQ Manager REST API with employee number and user type. IDM: if account absent, creates it; if present, updates permissions. IDM returns success or failure to Workato.
# On IDM success: Workato closes NSC ticket automatically with success note. User is active in Wiser with SSO login.
# On IDM failure or timeout: Workato fetches IDM/IntegrationHub logs, attaches to NSC ticket, adds failure comment, assigns ticket to GSD. Ticket remains open.

# u_wiser_actimize_baseline (750), u_wiser_ps_baseline (166), u_wiser_cx_baseline (928) added to sys_properties (NSC). Configurable by System Administrator without code deployment or Workato recipe republication.
# OI-01: Baseline exceeded — silent block or notify approver? Must be decided before PBI-04 (Workato recipe) can be built.
# OI-02 (CRITICAL): Full IDM API contract required from Ivan Sherbinin / Swapnil: endpoint URL, auth method, payload fields, response schema, error codes, SLA.
# OI-03: How is active Wiser user count per division retrieved? Wiser API (Rivka to confirm), NSC table, or IDM query?

# OI-04: Does existing NSC catalog item capture employee number and user type as form fields? If not, fields must be added before integration can function.",,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,2026-04-15 10:10:25.488,,,,"15/Apr/26 10:10 AM;6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76;*Design Document Generated — IT-33005: Automatic User Creation in Wiser*
Design document v1.0 (Draft) has been generated and is ready for attachment. The document covers:

* *10 Functional Requirements* (FR-01 to FR-10): catalog item, Workato trigger, reference user validation, license limit enforcement, conditional approval, IDM provisioning, success/failure handling, SSO verification, audit logging
* *6 Non-Functional Requirements*: performance, availability, security, compliance, scalability, maintainability
* *Solution Architecture*: Workato as middleware between NSC, Wiser API, and IDM/NetIQ Manager
* *9-Step Process Flow* with data flow and state transition tables
* *8 PBIs* broken down by team with dependencies
* *10 Open Issues* (OI-01 to OI-10) requiring stakeholder input
* *10 Test Scenarios* covering happy paths, error conditions, and edge cases

*Critical Open Issue (OI-10):* Discrepancy between the SOP (fully automated approval for all user types) and the Technical Design field (approval required for Fulfiller/Manager types) — requires stakeholder clarification before development.
Sources analyzed: CR description, Technical Design field, Wiser_Automation_SOP (2).pdf attachment, Wiser Guide for CEA Integrations (SharePoint), Wiser-Jira Workato Integration design (IT-6837).

The issue is happening during the TLS handshake because fedramp servers are FIPS‑enabled at the OS level. This means they only allow TLS 1.2 with specific FIPS‑approved cipher suites.Please ensure that the calling function in wiser is configured to use:
Cipher suites supported under FIPS (AES‑GCM based)
If wiser is using .NET, please make sure TLS 1.2 is explicitly enabled:
Older frameworks may require updates before TLS 1.2 works properly.
I believe ,Once TLS 1.2 with the correct cipher suites is enabled at wiser end, the connection should work.

Michael outlined the purpose of the meeting, which is to request a proactive report to identify and resolve rates and charges errors (101 and 102) before the billing cycle begins. Addressing errors in advance aims to smoothen the billing process.

Michael emphasized the importance of generating a report that highlights errors before the billing cycle, as the current reports only capture errors after the cycle runs. This proactive measure will allow for timely corrections.

Swati explained that error code 101 occurs when no recurring charge campaign is available, typically due to manual errors by agents. She suggested generating a report to check if scheduled campaigns or rate plans are mapped to products and sending it to the concerned team for fixing.
* *Cause of Error 101:* Missing scheduled campaigns or rate plans due to manual agent errors.
* *Proposed Solution:* Generate a report to identify such issues before the billing cycle, ensuring they are corrected beforehand.
Swati further explained that error code 102 arises when a scheduled campaign is defined but lacks a set date. This causes errors for all accounts linked to the product mapped to that campaign.
* *Cause of Error 102:* Scheduled campaigns without a set date.
* *Proposed Solution:* Apply logic to ensure all SKUs are defined inside the scheduled campaign with correct dates, preventing errors.

It was discussed the need to schedule the error reports to run before each billing cycle. Swati proposed automating the process by creating a job for this, while Michael suggested running the reports on a weekly basis rather than daily to avoid redundancy.
* *Agreement:* Reports will be scheduled weekly, not daily, to identify and correct errors before the billing cycle.
Michael inquired about the time required to create these reports. Swati will check with the team and provide an update by the end of the day or tomorrow.
Dana requested that Swati prepare a small design document outlining the job, its frequency, and execution details for documentation purposes. Swati agreed to provide this before starting the task.
Manimaran agreed to the weekly job proposal, suggesting it be scheduled for Fridays or Sundays. They also mentioned the possibility of manually creating the job if required.
Michael thanked everyone for their contributions and stated that the meeting minutes would be shared afterward. The meeting ended with all participants expressing their gratitude.
# *Error Report Generation:* Create a report to identify errors 101 and 102 before the billing cycle and forward it to the concerned team for resolution. (_Assigned to Swati_)
# *Error Report Scheduling:* Schedule the error reports to run weekly to identify and fix errors before the billing cycle. (_Assigned to Swati_)
# *Report Design Documentation:* Prepare a design document detailing the job, frequency, and execution plan. (_Assigned to Swati_)
# *Effort Estimation:* Check with the team and provide an estimate of the time required to create the error reports with the specified logic. (_Assigned to Swati_)
Please let me know if there are any additional points or changes you’d like to discuss.
Thank you for your attention to these items.

PTF OLA - impact change,IT-20512,773471,Change Request,In Testing,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P2,,Yana Gurevich,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,Reut Sagi,70121:b17a5a27-67c5-4f03-a3fb-16001ddbf256,Reut Sagi,70121:b17a5a27-67c5-4f03-a3fb-16001ddbf256,22/Aug/24 10:24 AM,15/Jun/26 3:36 PM,,,October 2024,,,,,0,On_Hold,,,"When the impact is getting lower, Wiser is working well, like the example you shared

Waiting for examples from Henry Miles.","09/Apr/25 2:52 PM;621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c;Hi @Henry Miles,
Implementing tax configuration at provider-based logic will require more time, and require lots of validation.

Instead, we are considering adding a new option for ""Canada (Non-Taxable)"" country. And put all the Canadian Accounts into that, that are not assigned with a Provider. This will allow accounts that do not require calling the SureTax API to be categorized under this option.
This change aims to simplify the process.
Please let us know your thoughts.
Shanta","22/Apr/25 1:53 PM;621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c;I have attached a PowerPoint that explains how taxes are done during invoice generation. Please review and also see our answers below.
This would be helpful, however it still does not prevent other countries from being sent through. Is there any workaround for other providers outside of Canada?

*As explained before, accounts that are set with taxable country of Countries that are added under ""Company Tax Method"" with ""Tax Method"" as SureTax, only would be sent to SureTax API for tax calculations.*
*Currently it is set with countries USA and Canada. So other countries are not set to SureTax.*

The issue is two-fold, 1) is the taxes that are being charged on Canadian accounts that shouldn’t be, but 2) is the fact that sending through customers in other countries shows up as revenue going through Suretax, even if ST doesn’t rate or return any taxes on the invoices. It still counts as revenues Suretax see on their end, and that in turn increases the total amount of our annual agreement (and overages) that we have with Suretax – our annual agreement is based on total dollar amount sent through for processing.

*For this reason, we have suggested to add a new country by name ""Canada (Non-Taxable)"" and put all the non-taxable Canadian Accounts into that, that are assigned with a Provider. And these accounts would not be sent through SureTax API. And rest of the Canadian accounts only will be sent for SureTax processing.*
Any other workarounds or ideas for how to prevent this?
*The approach suggested above is a good solution.*

I believe we can manually exempt individual accounts in CB so they don’t go through the API, but this will require a lot of manual work any time a new account is set up, and could also cause issues if accounts switch between providers (from US billing to international or vice versa).

*While setting tax exemption at the account level is an option, it is also possible that the setting may get missed for international accounts. That’s why ""Canada (Non-Taxable)"" would be the best option. We can also setup a job to automatically set the taxable country to “Canada (Non-Taxable)” for international accounts, as a failover option to catch all those accounts that were not correctly setup with taxable country.*
We have decided to continue with the proposal below and the following
# A new country by name ""Canada (Non-Taxable)"" and put all the non-taxable Canadian Accounts into that, that are assigned with a Provider.
# Script to change the Canadian accounts without a provider to Canada (Non-Taxable)

# A job to run before the invoice cycle to verify all accounts are set correctly before Surtax API is used.","28/Apr/25 12:58 PM;621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c;[^Design Document for SureTax Canada(Non-Taxable).docx]

# MIS - CAP Integration Scripted REST API — CreateStandardChange action extended to accept an optional 'ImpactCIs' array (list of CI FQDNs or sys_ids) in the request payload.
# On receipt of ImpactCIs, API calls existing RetrieveCIInstanceDetails CI-lookup logic to fetch all CIs related to the provided InstanceID. Each submitted CI is validated against this list.

# If any CI fails validation: API returns ReturnCode ERROR, IntegCode ERROR, IntegError naming the invalid CI(s) with reason. CR is NOT created.
# ImpactCIs is optional — callers that omit the field retain existing CreateStandardChange behaviour unchanged (backward compatible).

Request to create a *READ‑ONLY services account* that will allow pulling data from the CMDB via API.
* The Services Program team currently performs *manual exports* from the CMDB to maintain updated server lists.
* This involves manual comparison scripts, manual updates to hosts.yml, and data inconsistencies
* The lack of automated CMDB access makes the process time‑consuming and error‑prone.
* A *READ‑ONLY service account* with access to CMDB data.
* Ability for this account to be used to query CMDB
* Enable automated updates to server/host tables used by the CI/CD pipeline.
* Remove the need for manual extracts and Python comparison scripts.
* Ensure accuracy for ongoing reporting requests coming from R&D, Product, AppOps, and other teams.

Triage NSC Tickets Agent,IT-31777,842306,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,10/Feb/26 3:06 PM,09/Jun/26 12:31 PM,27/May/26 7:43 PM,09/Jun/26 12:31 PM,April 2026,,,,,0,,,,"h2. Requirements

The *NSC Tickets Agent* (Incident or Request) enables BA CS and BA Cadebill teams to resolve NSC tickets faster by retrieving historically similar tickets from ServiceNow and presenting AI‑assisted resolution recommendations.
The agent analyses and classifies the ticket, assigns it to a generic user, automatically updates its status, and based on the ticket description, generates a structured set of recommended insights.
* When an Incident or Request is assigned to the BA CS, BA Cadebill, or BA Fin Cadebill teams, the agent automatically classifies the ticket and assigns it to the designated user (Eitan Fireberg).
* The agent updates the ticket status to “Pending Future Activities” and sets a target date two weeks from the creation date.
* The agent changes the ticket status to “Pending Future Activities” with a Target Date of 2 weeks ahead.
* The agent is integrated with all NSC tickets managed by these teams.

* Upon assignment, the agent searches for historically similar NSC tickets and provides references to related cases, along with recommended next steps or resolution actions. These insights are automatically added to the ticket’s Work Notes.

# Business Rule 'WebhookNSC' fires on Incident and RITM tables when assignment_group changes to BA CS (df49d3554f615240eeeefcb28110c7cb), BA Cadebill (f607636d47bd5e10a95e4c2f316d4368), or BA Fin Cadebill (1905e050470d7210a95e4c2f316d43fc). Sends JSON payload {number, short_description, sys_id} via REST Message 'n8n nsc agent' / POST N8N to https://apimdev.nice.com/n8n-webhook/servicenow-case-search. Writes response body to current.work_notes. Errors caught and logged to syslog (source: 'NSC integration').

# Assigned To set to Eitan Fireberg; State set to 'Pending Future Activities'; Target Date set to creation date + 14 days; Additional Comments set to 'We will check and update'. Applied to both Incident and RITM ticket types.

# n8n workflow receives webhook, searches NSC tickets opened in last 3 years for up to 5 most similar results. Similarity fields: Incident — short_description, description, close_notes; RITM — additional details, comments, additional_comments, work_notes.

# Knowledge sources also searched: 6 Teams SharePoint channels (Wiser Knowledge, BP Certification in Wiser, Wiser-Jira Project, Wiser Releases, Wiser Activities, Billing IT), HowTo guides, ServiceNow code artifacts (Business Rules, Client Scripts, Script Includes, Flows, UI Actions), and business process docs at onexpert.cxoneexpert.ai/Process_Library.

# Workflow returns structured Work Notes: 'Similar <N> NSC Tickets Identified:' with up to 5 Incident/RITM numbers + 'Recommended Action:' consolidated resolution summary. If no matches: 'No similar NSC tickets were found for this incident.'

Create Integration from Cadebill to Salesforce,IT-29636,826129,Story,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Jonathan Jensen,712020:4c7bb93a-69d9-47b6-bc60-f97d9b955661,Augmon Jolley,712020:a8ec317d-0071-4db4-86b1-2dc3cf30ef96,Augmon Jolley,712020:a8ec317d-0071-4db4-86b1-2dc3cf30ef96,14/Nov/25 8:17 PM,10/Jun/26 4:36 AM,03/Jun/26 12:23 PM,01/Jun/26 8:59 PM,,,,,,0,MCL,,,"*Project Overview* [+video+|https://slack-files.com/T01LLJYQB0C-F09TF5N1B4H-089d4304a5] for more context

Our current sales process faces a key challenge: the *quantity of products* is tracked in our billing system, *Cadebill*, but our sales platform, *Salesforce CPQ*, continues to use the original quantity from the initial contract. This discrepancy leads to inaccurate renewals and amendments. To resolve this, we will implement a data synchronization process to pull the most current product usage data from Cadebill and update the corresponding Salesforce subscription records. This ensures that our renewal and amendment quotes are always based on the most up-to-date usage quantities.",,Augmon Jolley,Meital Rashti,,,,712020:a8ec317d-0071-4db4-86b1-2dc3cf30ef96,70121:33c9c08c-01ca-4a34-ac93-994a2946b894,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,10/Jun/26 4:36 AM;ug:09c91872-a878-4f07-97a1-3f1811d26aca;RE_ Requesting CCR for IT-29636-- Create Integration from Cadebill to Salesforce PlayVox .msg;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/189754,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,{},,,,,,Yes,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:
* A dedicated integration will be developed by the *Integration Team*.
* This integration will pull updated usage quantity data from *Cadebill* to *Salesforce*.
* The integration must specifically update two standard Salesforce CPQ fields on the {{SBQQ__Subscription__c}} object. These fields are:
** {{SBQQ__Quantity__c}}: This field is used by CPQ to determine the quantity for new quote lines during an amendment.
** {{SBQQ__RenewedQuantity__c}}: This field is used by CPQ to determine the quantity for a renewal quote.
** Cadebill_CPID__c field will be how this is mapped
*** The CP ID field looks to products and not subscription so if multiple subs have the same product - The CPI IDs would all match. To account for this:
**** Pull all the subscription lines of the same CP ID on a contract → sum up qty (if not terminated - checked by subscription.terminated_date) → update the qty of 1 subscription with the difference
***** This would gather the sum qty correctly and then add that to one subscription record
* The data must be synchronized on a *monthly* basis, specifically for contracts where the usage quantity has changed.
* The existing field, {{Original_Quantity__c}}, on the Subscription Object already captures the quantity the user originally enters on the Quote.

* The {{Original_Quantity__c}} field must be *excluded* from the Cadebill to Salesforce data synchronization process. Its purpose is to preserve the baseline quantity for historical reference and should never be overwritten by usage data.
* The *Integration Team* will configure the data sync from Cadebill to Salesforce
* The integration should be configured to update only the following two standard fields on the {{SBQQ__Subscription__c}} object:
* When a contract is created, the {{Original_Quantity__c}} field on the related subscription is automatically populated with the quantity from the original quote line.
* The Cadebill integration successfully sends updated usage quantities to Salesforce.
* The integration *only* updates the {{SBQQ__Quantity__c}} and {{SBQQ__RenewedQuantity__c}} fields on the subscription record.
* When a user amends a contract, the new quote line for the subscription product correctly reflects the updated {{SBQQ__Quantity__c}} from the subscription record.
* When a user renews a contract, the new quote line for the subscription product correctly reflects the updated {{SBQQ__RenewedQuantity__c}} from the subscription record.
* The data sync from Cadebill to Salesforce is successfully configured and running on a *monthly schedule* to update {{SBQQ__Quantity__c}} and {{SBQQ__RenewedQuantity__c}} on the Subscription Object

We have been in discussions about the design and wanted to add a field that signifies that the Cadebill team updated the Subscription. We want to use a check box field that is defaulted to FALSE in Salesforce but is set to TRUE when Cadebill pushes updated quantity data

|Subscription Object|{{Cadebill Quantity Updated}}|Checkbox field|Purpose: Default to FALSE and set to TRUE by the Cadebill Integration team when they update Subscriptions to trigger our flow|",,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,2026-05-24 05:29:35.694,1_*:*_1_*:*_10010963603_*|*_10001_*:*_1_*:*_10041_*|*_10006_*:*_1_*:*_6438115169_*|*_10005_*:*_1_*:*_0,,,"21/Nov/25 9:07 PM;712020:a8ec317d-0071-4db4-86b1-2dc3cf30ef96;[~accountid:712020:4c7bb93a-69d9-47b6-bc60-f97d9b955661] I don’t know if you guys have started this yet but I added an ‘Additional Requirement' at the bottom of the description.

We have tested all the invoice cycles for +June,2025+ invoice date in CPQa instance. We are attaching the validation report for your reference.
Report contains the *_‘Match’_* and *_‘Mismatch’_* data for all the invoice cycles.
* By *_‘Match’_* it mean quantities are matching and tier values are picked correctly.

* By ‘*_Mismatch’_* it mean quantities are not matching, but tier values are picked correctly.  There could be several reasons for quantities not matching for e.g.. change in feed, change in calculation logic etc. Reason for mismatch is added in the report.
In all the cases, +tiers values are picked correctly and this was the whole point of testing+. Please review and provide your feedback on the validation report.
Once everything is finalized, we can deploy the changes in PROD after 21^st^ billing cycle is completed.
@Eitan Fireberg: Please schedule a meeting so that we can discuss on the changes i.e.. creating the new tier related scheduled campaigns per currency and tier and adding tiers in the tier definition.

As you are aware, NICE custom tier logic doesn’t support *‘Taper’* pricing. Custom tier definition says it should calculate as per ‘Taper’ pricing but it calculate tiers are per tier(fixed) pricing. We have done all the validation with tier(fixed) pricing.

Moving ahead, we want to know how you want to proceed with it. Cadebill native logic support ‘*_Taper_*’ pricing. So if ‘*_Taper_*’ definition is mapped now, you will see calculation as per Taper pricing (you will see difference in numbers).

Do you want to keep existing account as per Tier pricing and  taper pricing for all the new accounts? Let us know your suggestions and thoughts..",25/Nov/25 3:05 AM;621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c;Robert: I've reviewed the mismatches and approve.,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Done,14/Dec/25 4:50 AM

Enhancement to CSM Assignment Logic in Wiser,IT-27357,810199,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Yana Gurevich,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,06/Aug/25 9:38 AM,07/Sep/25 7:06 AM,,07/Sep/25 7:06 AM,September 2025,,,,,0,,,,"*Current Behavior:*

Currently, if a solution-level CSM field is left blank, the system automatically inherits the Customer-level CSM. This behavior prevents users from intentionally leaving the solution-level CSM field empty in cases where no CSM should be assigned to a specific solution.

There is a business scenarios where a Customer-level CSM does not cover all solutions under the account. In such cases, need the ability to leave the solution-level CSM field blank to indicate that no CSM is assigned. The current logic does not support this and automatically overwrites the blank field with the Customer-level CSM. If the CSM field Manually removed - need to remain blank.

If the CSM field Manually removed - need to remain blank",,Linoy Tsarfaty,Rivka Eisenstein,Yana Gurevich,,,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,62c291d8a7de1e7c7c2cdb87,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,,,,,,,,0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,19/Aug/25 7:16 AM;3c42a289-0c07-4f7a-93df-af176dca609c;IT-27357 - Enhancement to CSM Assignment Logic in Wiser.docx;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/163190,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,SNOW Dev,,,,,,,Yana Gurevich,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Customer Support,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Rivka Eisenstein,62c291d8a7de1e7c7c2cdb87,,{},,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

Clarify Hybrid Case Closure Process When Customer Requests Closure,IT-27323,810027,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Meital Rashti,70121:33c9c08c-01ca-4a34-ac93-994a2946b894,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,05/Aug/25 12:11 PM,09/Sep/25 8:50 AM,,09/Sep/25 8:50 AM,September 2025,,,,,0,Silverstorm,,,"*Background*: When a customer requests case closure, Wiser sets the ""Customer Closure Requested"" flag to true. ** VM Management - Decommission VM assigned to *Nexidia-IT-VM*
** Server Management - Decommission Physical Server assigned to *Nexidia-IT-Server*

* Once those 3 Child SRs are resolved, 2 new SRs are automatically created
** Storage Management - Decommission Storage or Backup assigned to *Nexidia-IT-Storage*
** {color:#bf2600}Security System Management - Decommission Security Component assigned to {color}{color:#bf2600}*Nexidia-IT-Security - > MOVE UP*{color}
* Once those 2 Child SRs are resolved, one new SR is created

* Once that Child SR is resolved, workflow is complete and:
** {color:#4c9aff}Generate a destruction certificate – if its marked as Yes (new ticket) – Chris S (Ethan was generating in the past) - ON HOLD{color}
** {color:#bf2600}The parent gets moved to ""Implemented"" and status is updated to “Resolved”. No one is assigned the parent SR. Assignee - {color}Ben Loch.

Parent Decommission Ticket is created and assigned to Global-Nexidia-Decommission IT. 3 Child SRs automatically created
* Data Services - Decommission Existing Environment assigned to *Global-Nexidia-DataServices – if at least one CI is DB CI*
* Network Management- Decommission External Access assigned to *Nexidia-IT-Network – Yes*
* DevOps Monitoring - Decommission Existing Environment assigned to *Global-Nexidia-DevOps-Monitoring - Yes*
* Storage Management – *Turn off* Server Backups assigned to Nexidia-IT-Storage –  *NET NEW SR Created*  - *Yes*
* Once all 4 Child SRs above are resolved, 1 new Child SRs are created
** Server Management - Decommission Physical Server assigned to *Nexidia-IT-Server - yes*
* Once those 2 Child SRs are resolved, 2 new SRs are automatically created
** Storage Management - Decommission Storage or Backup assigned to *Nexidia-IT-Storage - yes*
** Security System Management - Decommission Security Component assigned to *Nexidia-IT-Security - yes*
* Once those 2 Child SRs are resolved, one new SR is created
** Network Management - Decommission Network Component assigned to *Nexidia-IT-Network - yes*
* Once that Child SR is resolved, workflow is complete
** The parent gets moved to ""Implemented"" and status is updated to “Resolved”. No one is assigned the parent SR. > even when a child is closed partially or cancelled.
* Once the SR is closed the system will change the state of the choose CI’s to “decommissioned”.

Require Status Change When "Restored" Checkbox is Unchecked,IT-26071,788161,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Yana Gurevich,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,12/Jun/25 5:55 PM,10/Aug/25 7:51 AM,,10/Aug/25 7:51 AM,August 2025,,,,,0,,,,"h3. *Require Status Change When ""Restored"" Checkbox is Unchecked*
Currently, when the ""Restored"" checkbox is unchecked on critical cases, the case status remains as ""Restored,"" which can lead to inconsistencies in SLA tracking and case handling.
To improve this behavior, we propose the following change:
* *Validation*: If the case status is still set to ""Restored,"" prompt the user to change the status before allowing the checkbox to be unchecked.

* *Purpose*: This ensures that engineers cannot uncheck the ""Restored"" checkbox while the case is still in ""Restored"" status, enforcing a proper status transition (e.g., to ""WIP"" or another appropriate status).

Engineers must update the case status from ""Restored"" to another valid status before they can uncheck the ""Restored"" checkbox. This prevents logical inconsistencies and ensures accurate SLA tracking.",,Alon Hefetz,Linoy Tsarfaty,Yana Gurevich,,,621f1bd06a4c4c0070adaae7,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,,,,,,,,0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,10/Aug/25 7:51 AM;ug:328640ee-937b-4119-be77-4336a2b848c6;IT-26071 - Require Status Change When Restored Checkbox is Unchecked.xml;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/162801,29/Jul/25 10:12 PM;3c42a289-0c07-4f7a-93df-af176dca609c;IT-26071-Require Status Change When .docx;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/162038,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,SNOW Dev,,,,,,,Yana Gurevich,6335750888ed2ebef97ca2a5,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Customer Support,Linoy Tsarfaty,6114ec62eb7aef006967538b,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,1.0,Alon Hefetz,621f1bd06a4c4c0070adaae7,,{},,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

Setup à General Settings à Cadebill Settings à Messages

# {color:#ff5630}Ability to receive Daily Usage Feed by customer by product {color}{color:#ff5630}_(need to build data feed)_{color}
# {color:#ff5630}Ability to receive a daily flat-file usage feed into a separate usage table in Cadebill{color}
# {color:#ff5630}Ability to track Rollover Minutes by customer by month {color}{color:#ff5630}_(need to build Rollover Ledger by customer)_{color}
# {color:#ff5630}Rollover Minutes = Prior Month Rollover Balance + Monthly Subscription Quantity - Usage{color}
# {color:#ff5630}Ability to Zero-out the Rollover Balance at the end of the annual Contract Term{color}
# {color:#ff5630}Ability to include Rollover Balance on customer's invoice (balance after current invoice usage){color}
# {color:#ff5630}Ability to calculate overage charges as a separate SKU: Overage quantity * overage rate {color}{color:#ff5630}_(need to build custom quantity calc)_{color}
# {color:#ff5630}Overage quantity exists when Usage > Monthly Subscription Quantity + Rollover Balance{color}
# {color:#bf2600}Creating a process to pull usage file from a dedicated table ( &job to pull it). This will be a dedicated usage table.{color}
# Ability to create a separate Overage Invoice +In Arears+ for customers with overage charges _(need new invoice cycle and ability to determine which customers have Overage with overage charges)_
# Invoice date = last day of month
# Customers with Overage charges will receive 2 invoices in month; 1 to Pre-Pay for Package and 1 to pay last month's Overage at end of month

# The 2 charges (Subscription and Overage) may be able to exist in a single customer invoice +*IF*+ Rev Recognition can properly separate revenue by appropriate revenue month AND customers agree with combining both charges on the same invoice.
# Ability to include Subscription SKU in the Minimum +*IF*+ that is a business requirement (sell VoApps Subscription on contract with CX products; Hybrid deal) _(existing functionality)_
+*Simplification Options - planned option instead of #7 above:*+
* Billing Subscriptions in Arears with Overage on the same invoice would eliminate need for 2nd invoice.  This is how we manage Package with Overages in CX today.
# VoApps has 4 SKUs, invoices 250 Accounts monthly with ~$1M/month in revenue
# SKUs:  Subscription SKU, Overage SKU, Implementation SKU and Professional Recording
# Approx 50% of VoApps customer receive an Overage Invoice per month
# Approx 63% of VoApps customers utilize Rollover minutes
# Currently, VoApps calculates and utilizes daily invoiceable amounts (daily usage net of rollover * rate) by customer for Sales, Marketing and Financial reporting during the month.
## {color:#bf2600}Cadebill has the ability to provide daily invoiceable amounts but need to create a daily job and create a report in current Reporting Tool.{color}

Motorola CR,IT-23655,782972,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,11/Mar/25 2:44 PM,30/Mar/25 11:20 AM,,30/Mar/25 11:20 AM,April 2025,,,,,0,,,,"After investigating, we found details about the integration between Motorola ServiceNow and Wiser (Michelle's design document is attached).
This integration allows the following actions:
* An API call is triggered from Motorola to Wiser with details for requesting the creation of a case. Wiser then creates the case and sends back the details.

* The next step involves comments. The business reason for the comments is to facilitate communication between NICE and Motorola. However, the issue is that every comment added to the case, whether internal or external, is marked as an external comment.
Another problem is that every comment received in their system creates an ""acknowledge"" notification in Wiser, which triggers another external comment sent to Motorola.
They have requested the following changes:
* Align the system so that only external comments are sent via API.
* Remove the ""acknowledge"" notification to prevent the addition of irrelevant comments to their ServiceNow.
On the technical side, we have reviewed the situation and can make these changes specifically for Motorola. As shown below, we can modify the call based on IP or name.
More than that after reviewing with Ricky (Rivka Beker) we understand that in the integration itself we are not indicating if the comment is internal or external, it happens in Wiser.

We will begin enforcing Multi-factor authentication (MFA) for non-SSO logins starting with the Now Platform^®^ Yokohama release planned for March 2025, which will enhance security and provide a more robust defense against potential security threats.

Beginning with the Now Platform^®^ Yokohama release planned for March 2025, Multi-factor authentication will be turned on by default in all instances. This initiative underscores our commitment to enhancing security across the platform. Please find key details below:
# *No Impact on SSO Logins and Integrations*
MFA enforcement will apply *exclusively* to username and password-based UI logins. Single Sign-On (SSO) logins and integrations remain unaffected.

Users performing username and password-based logins (excluding snc_external users) will be required to set up MFA. Upon their first successful local login after the Yokohama upgrade, each user will have a 30-day window to self-enroll in MFA.
Administrators will have the ability to modify the MFA policy to extend the enrollment window or exempt specific users, roles, or groups from the MFA requirement, if necessary.

Please review this [KB1700938|https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB1700938] or the [MFA FAQ|https://support.servicenow.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sysparm_article=KB1709783] for more details.

In addition, beginning with the Now Platform^®^ Australia release planned for March 2026, we will deprecate the glide.authenticate.multifactor property that allows customers to disable Multi-factor authentication.
*Here’s what we need you to do*
Start planning, you have two options:

* You can configure MFA before upgrading to Yokohama. Review the Multi-factor authentication (MFA) [+product documentation+|https://www.servicenow.com/docs/bundle/xanadu-platform-security/page/integrate/authentication/concept/mfa-config-landing.html] to learn how to configure MFA for your instances ahead of your migration to ensure the smoothest roll-out possible
* We will keep this Communication record open for 4 weeks to allow for any necessary documentation and planning.

Legacy Password Reset UI deprecation notification: Review

Our records indicate that you may use a feature which we will no longer deploy or support as of an upcoming Now Platform^®^ release. To ensure that your users continue to receive an optimized password reset user experience, please take a moment to review the information below.
* Beginning with the Now Platform^®^ Yokohama release planned for March 2025, we will no longer deploy or activate the feature listed below to new instances.

* Beginning with the Now Platform^®^ Australia release planned for March 2026, we will end support for the feature listed below. While it will remain active in your instance, including when you upgrade to a new release family, we will not be able to support it.

* Start planning. You have three options:

*** Use the latest [Password Reset experience|https://www.servicenow.com/docs/bundle/xanadu-servicenow-platform/page/administer/login/concept/exploring-password-reset.html] to reset passwords and review the [Product Documentation.|https://docs.servicenow.com/bundle/xanadu-servicenow-platform/page/administer/login/reference/password-reset-landing-page.html]

** Maintain the unsupported feature as custom code. We will not deactivate or remove the unsupported feature from your instance. You’ll still be able to access it as an active feature; however, it will be considered custom code, and we will not be able to support it.

** Contract with [ServiceNow|https://www.servicenow.com/services/overview.html] or a [ServiceNow Solution Partner|https://www.servicenow.com/partners/partner-finder.html] to assist with solutions. Contact them directly for service availability and cost.

CEA- CMDB Requirements,IT-21580,777904,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Meital Rashti,70121:33c9c08c-01ca-4a34-ac93-994a2946b894,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,04/Nov/24 3:36 PM,15/Dec/24 6:42 AM,,15/Dec/24 6:42 AM,December 2024,,,,,0,,,,"* Rack location is broken
** To get to it, go to Hardware > Assets
** When you select a rack location, it doesn’t save.
* Rename “IP Netmask” to “Subnet Mask” and move the other field under IP Address on CI Level
** Information is not being filled in now by CEA
** Wiser team will double check in the backend to verify it’s not used in other places
* Warranty Service Level has a duplicate field.
** *Remove duplicate field* - the top right one and *keep bottom*
* Update current field *server role list* on CI Level
** This will be long term solution to identify how to handle in the future if there are multiple
** Currently CEA doesn’t pick multiple values currently in spreadsheet
* Create new field *Application External IP address* on instance level
** Could be multiple application IP addresses. External IP that the customer uses
** Nice to have: field is validated it’s in an IP address format
** Do we need to remove the old one and add new one?
*** Keep an audit list but also to pull SRs for history
* *Update Additional URL field* on the instance level to a multipick up to 10 URLs
** Some customer have more than 2 URLs which is the current max
** Needs to be a column in the instance view for reports
* Create new field on the *instance level* call *VDI Pools*
** Front end access to back end environment
** Reportable – field in report by customer
** Being able to report on which pools are on which connection servers – relationship between VDI Connection Server and VDI Pool
** Add tab to instance for relationship and it will create a new column as well.
* Create new field on the *instance level* called *VDI connection servers*
** Same related tab for VDI Pool
** Being able to report on which pools are on which connection servers – *relationship between VDI Connection Server and VDI Pool*
** Add tab to instance for relationship and it will create a new column as well.
** You don’t have a VDI connection server without a VDI Pool
*** VDI Connection server could have multiple pools
* Create new field *Customer Connectivity Method* to the *instance level* instead of CI level
* Create new field called *Customer Authentication Method* to the instance level

* Remove SSO/LDAP field or repurpose this to the *Customer Authentication Method*",,Dany Ditchi,Meital Rashti,Rivka Eisenstein,,,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,70121:33c9c08c-01ca-4a34-ac93-994a2946b894,62c291d8a7de1e7c7c2cdb87,,,,,,,,0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,15/Dec/24 12:00 AM,,,,SNOW Dev,,,,,,,Meital Rashti,70121:33c9c08c-01ca-4a34-ac93-994a2946b894,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Customer Support,Dany Ditchi,633f26b4234d44d406d50578,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Rivka Eisenstein,62c291d8a7de1e7c7c2cdb87,,{},,Nexidia,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

column2: The TBU calculation is based all ENU SLA of the request while at least one SLA should be marked with 'Has Breached' True (including cancelled SLAs).
Following is the TBU calculation in Request table:

Sum up the “Business elapsed time” of all ENU SLA of the request (Business duration is the “Business elapsed time” from the Task SLA. It measures working hours’ time since the “Start time“ from the Task SLA).
Reduce the SLA Duration of the Breached SLA.
The TBU is:
The TBU represents 1 minus the deviation ratio between the time allocated to the SLA and the time it took.
When there is no deviation the TBU will be 1.
When there is a big deviation the TBU will be a number closer to zero.
When there is a small deviation the TBU will be a number closer to 1.",,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Done,08/Oct/24 10:58 AM

*Requirement 2:* Add an option to link an existing Jira ticket to a Service Request (SR) without automatically creating a new Jira ticket.

*Scenario:* In the current business process, DevOps teams often work on an issue and later open a Service Request (SR). They need the ability to link the existing Jira ticket to the SR to avoid creating duplicate Jira tickets.
*Proposed Solution:* Introduce a “Jira Existing?” flag. If set to “Yes,” the system should prompt the user to add the link to the existing Jira ticket.

I'll explain here the behavior of the case, so you don't need to read previous messages:
When Jeff approved the tasks, this business rule was triggered:
This BR puts the field u_downtime(System Down)=true, which triggers the next BR:
This BR sets the Restoration(u_dt_end) field, in this particular case to empty because of the conditions of the script.

As the Restoration field changes, the next BR is triggered: [https://nicetest.service-now.com/sys_script.do?sys_id=217618f81b628510ef55a7d5604bcb04|https://nicetest.service-now.com/sys_script.do?sys_id=217618f81b628510ef55a7d5604bcb04|smart-link]

This BR takes the differences between the start date and the restoration date, and set Impact duration field as the value of the difference, but since restoration was empty, the difference cannot be computed and an empty value in returned. Hence, the impact duration is also set ot empty.","19/Aug/24 1:40 PM;6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76;Suggested solution:

CS7133119 Solution Proposed: niceprod | P2 - High | Performance Impact - Users Affected,IT-17468,766397,Bug,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P2,Works as Designed,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,31/Jan/24 6:41 AM,18/Feb/25 10:28 AM,,18/Feb/25 10:28 AM,February 2024,,,,,0,,,,"|Please also check why our script didn’t catch this. I also didn’t understand what the user tried to do
We are placing this issue in Solution Proposed.
* The Default semaphore usage looks healthy and the internal alert has been cleared.
* Our internal monitoring system triggered the following alert "" Performance Impact - Users Affected - Default Semaphore Impacted niceprod""
* The instance stats intermittent spike in Default semaphore usage between 11:30 and 14:30 PST, Jan 30
* On further reviewing the issue, it appears the nodes had low memory conditions causing high instance response time.
* The cause of the low memory conditions on impacted nodes was due to long running /api/now/graphql transactions triggered by
* The user transaction /api/now/graphql was trying to process large amount of records ~1M on sys_audit_relation for document key: 038ff03b37f15200b7e9616043990e31 causing memory contention.

I’m reducing the audience a little bit. Reut, your suggestion to move all this into Wiser will not work, at least not for Cloud Operations. You cannot determine the assignment group based on the source of the alert. In other words, I cannot look at Pingdom as the source and then use that as the logic to say all Pingdom alerts will go to this group. We make very granular changes to individual alerts as to who they need to go to. So unless you want to hire an FTE whose only job is to come up with a few hundred assignment rules, I’d steer away from that proposal. Cloudwatch is another example. CloudWatch simply reflects a monitoring tool. It doesn’t dictate who gets the alert. Cloudwatch can identify a problem with an RDS service in which case it may go to the database team for some product or to the AppOps team for others, as different teams have different requirements as to who gets the alerts.

What I can suggest is that you may have logic that says if the tag WiserGroup in the alert does NOT exist, we can assign to certain groups based on the logic that other teams may be able to provide you. But if the tag does exist in the alert then the tag will have to be used to decide who to assign an alert/case to. It may be that other teams can break it up that way, but it will not work for us. But I doubt that you will be able to assign it correctly for other teams as well if you simply base it on the source of the alert.

# Consolidate gap: A case with a future meeting can't be consolidate, the meeting will have to be cancelled first. Once engineer tries to consolidate the case and there is a future meeting, he will see the following error message: “This case has a future meeting. In order to consolidate the case please cancel this meeting first”. Resolve action will be aborted.

# Consolidate gap: When invite was triggered by the Engineer (Invite is true, Meeting status is None), contact did not choose a slot yet, and the engineer tries to consolidate the case: Alert message (red message) will be displayed to the engineer:“Please note, an invite to a future meeting was sent to the customer. The invite will no longer be available”. ‘Invite’ field in ‘Outlook Meetings’ table will be updated to False. When the contact will click on the ‘Schedule a Meeting with Support’ link from the invite notification the system will open the ‘Schedule a Meeting with Support’ form and display the following message:“Meeting invite is no longer available”. Filter to display the message is: Invite = False, Meeting status is None, and case cancel, resolve, or convert.

Enabling the Delete option for Release Task in the Wiser Project Release. There are chances that the Release Task is created in another release and the check-ins are made against that task. There is no way to remove the Release Task from the Project Release entity. We have some pipelines and automation created based on the Release tasks associated to the project release and in case if someone mistakenly commits the changes against a Release Task which is not referring to the current project release, we need the capability to delete/dissociate the Release Task from the current project release. The Release Tasks should be allowed to be deleted from the Release Project if not required and if needed can be assigned to any other Wiser Project Release entity.

The Release Tasks once created and added in the Project Release can never be removed. The delete option is not enabled.",,Alon Hefetz,Naga Dakarapu,Nofar Plotkin,Snehal Patil,,621f1bd06a4c4c0070adaae7,628fdd90d5ec4f0069e99359,712020:18118181-6f32-4351-a220-79fd2aa2888b,6283698562e0790069a58cee,,,,,,,0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,27/Sep/23 11:23 AM;ug:4618850c-d06b-47ac-a3ce-1d970deeb28e;IT – 155429 - Enabling the Cancel option for Release Task in the Wiser Project Release (71bdd45c-a640-42c9-a18a-29887667e59d).docx;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/145199,23/Oct/23 8:55 AM;ug:328640ee-937b-4119-be77-4336a2b848c6;IT-15429  Create a new field on source control table.xml;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/145609,11/Sep/23 11:06 AM;d6fd1331-62c0-492f-9ac5-73bf4afa5cb2;Wiser Change Request - Release Task Deletion.docx;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/145002,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,23/Oct/23 12:00 AM,,,,SNOW Dev,,,,,,,Snehal Patil,6283698562e0790069a58cee,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Professional Services,Naga Dakarapu,628fdd90d5ec4f0069e99359,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Nofar Plotkin,712020:18118181-6f32-4351-a220-79fd2aa2888b,,{},,Actimize,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

glide.smtp.send_partial,IT-12941,754533,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P3,Done,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,08/Mar/23 12:03 PM,23/Apr/23 10:46 AM,,23/Apr/23 10:46 AM,April 2023,,,,,0,,,,"Create this new glide record for partial email send if there’s wrong email addresses in the recipients list

Splits outgoing email between valid and invalid recipients. The email is sent only to the valid recipients. A new email containing the invalid recipients is created in the *send-ready* state and attempts to send again.

If one of the recipients is not valid then the email record would be updated by the system with the state as ""Ready"" and Type as ""send-retry-delayed"".This is performed by the system in an attempt to reprocess the email.
Adding the following property 'glide.smtp.send_partial' with the value 'TRUE' should help to resolve the issue.
For more details on resolution refer KB below-
[KB1261730 : Email records are stuck in ""Ready"" state with Type as ""send-retry-delayed""|https://hi.service-now.com/kb?id=kb_article_view&sys_kb_id=ef5289991b7ce550546e35df034bcbda]

* when the button is clicked a validation message should appear “please confirm production is down​

* if Confirmed - the “Escalation Justification” test filed should be mandatory.
* the escalation flag & justification should integrate into Jira.

* R&D/product users are complaining CS users are not placing the attachments in the shared folder - the requests is:

New Template for Low Volume Functionality Cases - Workflow Health Check,IT-11780,751663,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P2,Done,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,Manny Shaw,62a1faf876c0360069f4e35c,Manny Shaw,62a1faf876c0360069f4e35c,21/Dec/22 4:35 PM,16/Jan/23 7:16 AM,,16/Jan/23 7:16 AM,January 2023,,,,,0,,,,"Template name is: *Low Volume – Workflow Health Check*
Cloud/hosted only. The template is to populate in the internal comments section (not external comments).

Once saved, the comment should be populated in the Internal Comments and an email sent to the following internal ppl: Jeffrey Strnad, Ofir Shemesh, Erik Sebold, Ajay Kumar, TAM (if exist at the Wiser), and all internal members mentioned in the “Internal List” of the case.  Subject line: Impact Case____, Customer, Low Volume Workflow Health Check” (case and customer populated automatically)
New Template text is below, where [   ] indicates automatically populated field:
A Low Volume [Impact] case [Case_____] has been created on [date time].
_“Workflow Health Check Validated - Case move to Monitor._
* _Based on 'Interactions Sent To Ingested' – What is last submitted date? How many hours since last activity? What is the threshold?_
* _Based on ‘Interactions Ingested’ – What is last ingested date? How many hours since last activity? What is the threshold?_
_2. ETL – Based on ‘NIA Jobs’ – Core Update ETL – ‘Last Success Date’ - When was the last successful ETL run? Higher than 4 hours?_
* _Based on Discovery\Update\ETL – ‘Last Success Date’ - When was the last successful ASR run? Higher than 4 hours?_
* _Based on Interaction ASR – ‘Hours Since Last Activity’ - When was the last successful ASR run per source? Higher than 4 hours?_
_4. DEU – Based on DEU report – What is Hours Since Last Activity? Does it miss the threshold (only on sources that have Threshold set)?_

_Next update ETA: In X hours.”_",,Manny Shaw,Rivka Eisenstein,,,,62a1faf876c0360069f4e35c,62c291d8a7de1e7c7c2cdb87,,,,,,,,,0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,16/Jan/23 7:15 AM;ug:10c4f7f2-1846-44c4-a603-a79a43979d0e;IT-11780-New Template for Low Volume Functionality Cases - Workflow Health Check.xml;https://nice-it.atlassian.net/rest/api/3/attachment/content/140757,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,SNOW Dev,,,,,,,Dana Trybiasz,6114ed43e6e6f80071784a76,,ApprovalsCFValue{approvals=[]},,,,,,No,,,,,,,,,,,,,Customer Support,Manny Shaw,62a1faf876c0360069f4e35c,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,Rivka Eisenstein,62c291d8a7de1e7c7c2cdb87,,{},,Enterprise,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"Sizing document change:

Actimize watch DataOps - email to case,IT-11131,750456,Change Request,Done,IT,IT,software,atlassianadmin,6124bbed3ef46a0070571c09,,,P1,Done,Eitan Fireberg,621f1c4f94f7e20069fdd50c,Ilan Schwartzman,621781d1ba649b006aad7e40,Ilan Schwartzman,621781d1ba649b006aad7e40,10/Nov/22 7:23 AM,21/Dec/22 6:27 AM,,21/Dec/22 5:56 AM,December 2022,,,,,0,Silverstorm,,,"We spoke about this several times in the past,

We have in Actimize Watch a team of ""DataOps"" these guys analyze the Actimize Watch product logs and nowadays they open a ticket in Jira by sending an email to Jira (BTW, Actimize Watch is a cloud product)
This is causing an operational challenge as the content should be addressed to the PS TAMs that work in Wiser.
The logs are per customer tenant – meaning Instance (kind of monitoring but that analysis is done manually for now)
In different scenarios, once the PS team completed the internal analysis and actions, they are required to expose the issue to the customer (externalize a case) – today it’s mainly done via emails…
I explored the option of opening a manual internal case manually.
It's causing other changes, such as,
# Each customer must have a team line project.
# We cannot leverage the ""externalize case"" functionality.
# The process of opening a case requires more effort than just sending an email…
I'm requesting to expand the existing ""email to case"" functionality that was developed for DataIQ to customize a solution for ""Actimize Watch""

By adding an additional platform defined as ""Actimize Watch"" and defining for the DataOps a unique email they should send the email from and defining the email subject mask to include the instance number – we can relatively easily provide a solution that is very similar to the way they work with Jira today.
